## Atoms.dev — Competitive Brief (2026-06-01)

**Run date:** 2026-06-01 | **Window:** 2026-05-25 → 2026-06-01 | **Baseline:** DEV-62 (2026-05-26)

---

### What It Builds
Full-stack web apps and SaaS products from a natural-language prompt, using a simulated team of 8 named AI agents (Iris: research; Emma: PM; Bob: architecture; Alex: engineering; Mike: lead; Sarah: SEO; David: data; Adrian: ads). Ships with auth, PostgreSQL, Stripe, SEO, and one-click deploy. Race Mode (Max plan): four AI models build simultaneously; user picks the best result. No material product change this week; blog has had no posts since May 5, 2026.

### Onboarding Flow
Prompt → Iris does pre-build market research → 8-agent team builds concurrently → one-click deploy to Atoms Cloud. Race Mode (Max): four model builds run simultaneously. No changelog or /whats-new surface (/changelog and /whats-new both 404 as of 2026-06-01).

### Pricing (2026-06-01)
| Plan | Price | Credits/mo | Storage | Notes |
|------|-------|------------|---------|-------|
| Free | $0 | 15/day (25/mo) | 2 GB | 2 Cloud projects |
| Pro | $20/mo | 100 | 10 GB | Private projects, custom domains |
| Max | $100/mo | 500 | 100 GB | Race Mode, higher accuracy |

Team Plan (Apr 2026) absent from public pricing page — likely still in limited beta.

### Strengths
1. **Named-agent UX transparency** — 8 visible specialist agents with defined roles create user trust: "a real team is working on this." No competitor shows this. [Source: atoms.dev homepage]
2. **Pre-build research gate (Iris)** — market research, competitive analysis, and audience sizing before any code runs. 4/6 competitors now ship some form of this. [Source: atoms.dev/blog]
3. **Race Mode** — 4 parallel model iterations; user picks best result. Quality multiplier unavailable in any other platform. [Source: atoms.dev/blog]

### Weaknesses
1. **No public changelog** — /changelog and /whats-new both 404; opacity about what changed makes trust-building difficult.
2. **Race Mode gated at $100/mo** — the quality multiplier is locked behind Max tier; Pro users get no parallel-model benefit.
3. **No Arabic/RTL support found** — zero evidence of MENA market consideration.

### Patterns to Copy
- **Named-agent UX** — surface agent roles (researcher, PM, architect, engineer, QA) visibly in our build interface; builds trust without adding friction.
- **Pre-build research gate** — before starting a build, run a lightweight market/competitive research step (what exists, who uses it, what differentiates it). Iris pattern.

### Patterns to Beat
- **Arabic/RTL gap** — Atoms ships no Arabic/RTL support; our MENA-native defaults are a structural competitive advantage in the Kuwait/GCC market.
- **Changelog opacity** — our transparent, queryable issue/comment history is a trust differentiator vs Atoms' 404 changelog.

### Decision
**Study only** — Named-agent UX and pre-build research gate are worth absorbing as prompt/UX patterns; no import or rebuild warranted. Race Mode is architecturally interesting but the $100 price point and closed API limit practical adoption.

### Sources
- https://atoms.dev
- https://atoms.dev/blog (last post May 5, 2026)
- https://github.com/FoundationAgents/MetaGPT
- https://toolworthy.ai (Atoms.dev analysis)

### Diff vs 2026-05-26 Baseline
- **new_features:** No material new features this week (no blog posts since May 5)
- **pricing_changes:** —
- **new_flows:** —
- **removed:** —
- **positioning:** Hero headline changed from "world's first AI software company" (MGX era) to "Turn ideas into products that sell / AI employees to validate ideas, build products, and acquire customers" — pivoting from tech identity to founder/operator GTM; 8th agent (David, Data Analyst) now explicitly listed on homepage separately from Adrian

## Antigravity 2.0 (Google) — Competitive Brief (2026-06-01)

**Run date:** 2026-06-01 | **Window:** 2026-05-25 → 2026-06-01 | **Baseline:** DEV-62 (2026-05-26, just 7 days after GA launch)

---

### What It Builds
Google's agentic developer platform — a VS Code-forked desktop IDE plus CLI, SDK, and Managed Agents API. Handles full-stack web apps, long-running automated workflows (dependency upgrades, security scans, multi-repo refactoring), and agentic CI/CD pipelines. Dynamic subagents run frontend/backend/testing in parallel; a browser subagent (live Chrome instance) visually verifies generated UI without manual DevTools. This week: post-launch stabilization (v2.0.6, v2.0.10 patches), Linux sandboxing extended, IDE integration button added.

### Onboarding Flow
Install Antigravity desktop app (macOS/Linux/Windows) → connect GitHub repo or Google Cloud workspace → prompt agent. Manager Surface: observe multiple agents across workspaces from one interface. Scheduled tasks: cron-based background agents for dependency scans, security sweeps. IDE integration button (v2.0.6): one-click open of current workspace in Antigravity IDE.

### Pricing (2026-06-01)
| Plan | Price | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|
| Free (Individuals) | $0 | 20 req/day (slashed from 250); full feature access |
| AI Pro (bundled) | ~$20/mo | Google AI Pro subscription |
| AI Ultra | $100/mo | 5× Pro limits |
| AI Ultra Premium | $200/mo | 20× Pro limits (was $250) |
| Enterprise | Custom | VPC, SSO, audit logs, BigQuery |

**Key change:** Free tier slashed from 250 → 20 req/day. $100 bonus credits promo expired May 25.

### Strengths
1. **Parallel dynamic subagents** — frontend/backend/testing agents run concurrently by default; A2A protocol enables inter-agent communication. Only platform in cohort to architect this at the OS level. [Source: google.com/antigravity, Google I/O 2026]
2. **Scheduled cron agents** — agents run background jobs on schedule (dependency scans, security sweeps) without user prompts. No competitor ships this as a first-class feature. [Source: Antigravity 2.0 docs]
3. **Google ecosystem depth** — native Firebase, Android, AI Studio, Google Cloud integrations; enterprise VPC/SSO/audit via Managed Agents API. [Source: Google I/O 2026 blog]

### Weaknesses
1. **Free tier gutted** — 250 → 20 req/day is a 92% reduction; makes experimentation impractical without paid plan.
2. **Post-launch stability** — v2.0.2–v2.0.9 were emergency patches (config wipes, data loss, SSH/WSL breakage from automatic upgrade); trust damage is real.
3. **No built-in deploy** — requires existing infra; contrast Lovable/Bolt one-click deploy.

### Patterns to Copy
- **Scheduled cron agents** — agents run on schedule for routine maintenance (dependency checks, security scans, changelog summaries). Directly applicable to our weekly sweep autopilot and QA automation.
- **A2A inter-agent protocol** — typed message contracts between agents enable handoff without free-text context passing.

### Patterns to Beat
- **Free tier gutted** — our accessible pricing (or volume-based model) is a competitive wedge for individual developers and small teams.
- **Desktop-only** — our browser-accessible agents vs Antigravity's install-required approach.

### Decision
**Study only** — Parallel subagent orchestration and scheduled cron agent patterns are architecturally worth studying; A2A protocol is directly applicable to our squad handoff model. Do not recommend Antigravity to clients until MCP auth fragmentation and the slashed free tier are addressed.

### Sources
- https://antigravity.google (or current URL)
- https://developers.google.com/antigravity (changelog)
- https://techcrunch.com (Google I/O 2026 coverage, May 19 2026)
- https://gradually.ai (Antigravity changelog tracker)

### Diff vs 2026-05-26 Baseline
- **new_features:** v2.0.6 IDE integration button (one-click open workspace in Antigravity IDE, May 22); v2.0.10 G1 credit bug fix + reliability (May 28); Linux sandboxing extended (previously macOS-only)
- **pricing_changes:** Free tier slashed 250 → 20 req/day; $100 bonus credits promo expired May 25
- **new_flows:** IDE integration button reduces context-switch friction
- **removed:** —
- **positioning:** Unchanged (professional developer platform, Google ecosystem depth)

# ADR-003 — Mobile Layer Default (Expo + EAS Build)

**Status:** Adopted  
**Date:** 2026-06-01  
**Deciders:** Solution Architect (solution-architect), CEO Command Squad  
**Extends:** ADR-001 (DEV-128) — adds the mobile layer omitted from the web-stack ADR  
**Supersedes:** —  
**Superseded by:** —

---

## Context

ADR-001 (DEV-128) established the default web stack (Next.js + Supabase + Vercel) but left the mobile layer undefined. The Android Attendance app (DEV-43, DEV-30) was built using Expo + local Gradle without a formal ADR backing that decision. The `android-apk-local-builder` workspace skill has accumulated `retry_log=3` QA gate failures (DEV-174), signalling that local APK builds are fragile in the current environment. Future mobile apps (HR mobile, delivery driver app) need a consistent, documented baseline to avoid stack divergence.

---

## Decision

The devosnt App Factory adopts **Expo (React Native)** as its default mobile framework with the following build split:

| Environment | Build system | Output |
|---|---|---|
| Development / local | `eas build --local` (Gradle) | Debug APK |
| Staging / preview | EAS Build cloud (free tier) | Signed internal APK |
| Production / release | EAS Build cloud | Signed release AAB / APK |

This decision covers Android-first mobile apps. iOS coverage via EAS Build is provisioned when the client supplies an Apple Developer account.

> **Interim rule (until DEV-174 is `done`):** the `eas build --local` path is considered unstable. All builds, including development, must use EAS Build cloud until the android-apk-local-builder skill is validated.

---

## Layer-by-Layer Rationale

### Framework — Expo (React Native)
- React Native shares ~90% of code with the web React codebase; the App Factory's agents already know React.
- Expo managed workflow eliminates native boilerplate: camera, notifications, location, and file system are SDK modules requiring no Xcode or Android Studio configuration.
- Expo SDK is compatible with the Supabase JS client — the same auth library used in the web stack.
- TypeScript end-to-end consistency with Next.js frontend; shared types across layers are feasible.
- Expo SDK ships quarterly releases with documented upgrade tooling (`npx expo upgrade`).

### Build — EAS Build (Expo cloud CI) for staging and production
- Managed signing: Android keystores stored in Expo's secure vault; no local keystore management.
- Reproducible build environment: Docker-based, pinned to exact Expo SDK + Node + Gradle versions.
- OTA updates via Expo Updates: JS-layer fixes ship without a new APK Play Store review cycle.
- EAS Submit automates Play Store upload.
- Free tier: 30 builds/month; production apps move to EAS Production plan.

### Build — `eas build --local` for development (when DEV-174 is resolved)
- Zero cloud cost and no queue wait during fast iteration.
- Requires: pinned `JAVA_HOME`, `GRADLE_VERSION` in `eas.json`, and a Docker-based local environment.
- Gated on DEV-174 fix; not used in any new app until the skill passes its QA gate.

---

## When to Deviate

### Deviate to native Android (Kotlin) when ANY of the following apply:
1. **AR/VR or computer vision** requiring custom OpenGL/Vulkan pipelines or camera below 30 fps.
2. **Bluetooth LE / NFC POS integrations** with no Expo SDK module and no viable community module.
3. **Heavy background processing** (continuous GPS at <1 s interval, audio streaming) that Expo background tasks cannot support.
4. **Security hardware modules** (HSM, Secure Enclave beyond standard biometrics).
5. **Client mandate**: client explicitly requires a native Kotlin codebase with in-house mobile engineers.

### Deviate to Web PWA (Next.js) instead of a native app when:
- App is informational / read-heavy with occasional form input.
- No native device APIs required (no camera, no push notifications, no offline-first data).
- Time-to-market ≤ 2 weeks and the client cannot manage app store distribution.

All deviations require a written ADR amendment approved by the Solution Architect and CEO Command Squad **before** any build work starts.

---

## Minimum Viable Mobile Build Checklist

Every Expo app must pass this checklist before the QA gate (`in_review` → `done`):

### Environment variables
- [ ] `EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` — Supabase project URL
- [ ] `EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` — Supabase anon/public key
- [ ] `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL` — set only if API is not Supabase direct
- [ ] Secrets (service role key, payment keys) stored in **EAS Secrets vault**, never in `.env` or `app.config.js`

### `app.config.js` / `app.json`
- [ ] `expo.android.package` set to `com.newtechkw.<appname>` (reverse domain)
- [ ] `expo.version` and `expo.android.versionCode` bumped for every release build
- [ ] `expo.plugins` lists all native Expo SDK modules used

### `eas.json`
- [ ] `development` profile: `"buildType": "apk"`, `"distribution": "internal"`
- [ ] `preview` profile: `"buildType": "apk"`, `"distribution": "internal"`, `"env"` block for staging Supabase URL
- [ ] `production` profile: `"buildType": "app-bundle"`, `"distribution": "store"`, `"autoIncrement": true`

### Signing (production only)
- [ ] Android keystore generated and stored via `eas credentials` (EAS vault)
- [ ] SHA-1 / SHA-256 fingerprints documented in the build issue metadata (`signing_fingerprint`)
- [ ] Keystore backed up to client's 1Password vault or equivalent

### QA gate items (mobile-specific)
- [ ] Build succeeds via `eas build --profile preview --platform android`
- [ ] Auth flow tested on physical device or emulator API 33+
- [ ] OTA update channel configured for production (`"channel": "production"` in `eas.json`)
- [ ] No hardcoded URLs or secrets in JS bundle (grep check: `grep -rE "(supabase|_key|secret|password)" src/`)
- [ ] App cold-start < 3 s on mid-range Android (2 GB RAM)

---

## Reversal Conditions

This ADR is reconsidered if **any** hold:

1. Expo SDK drops React Native support or licence changes post-acquisition.
2. EAS Build cloud cost exceeds $500/month workspace-wide (evaluate self-hosted Turtle CLI).
3. More than 50% of new app requests require features on the deviation list above.
4. React Native's New Architecture (Fabric + JSI) causes breaking regressions across 2+ consecutive Expo SDK upgrades.

---

## Validation Path

- **DEV-174** (android-apk-local-builder skill fix) is the active validation task. This ADR's `eas build --local` path is gated on DEV-174 reaching `done`.
- When DEV-174 closes, the Solution Architect must update the **Interim rule** section above to remove the restriction.

---

## References

- Expo SDK docs: https://docs.expo.dev
- EAS Build: https://docs.expo.dev/build/introduction/
- EAS Credentials (managed signing): https://docs.expo.dev/app-signing/managed-credentials/
- React Native New Architecture: https://reactnative.dev/docs/the-new-architecture/landing-page
- DEV-43: Android Attendance build — Expo + Gradle precedent
- DEV-30: Architecture: Android Attendance — first Expo usage
- DEV-128: ADR-001 — Default Tech Stack (this ADR extends it)
- DEV-174: android-apk-local-builder skill fix (validation path, gated)
issues.title: ADR-003 — Mobile Layer Default (Expo + EAS Build)

## Replit Agent — Competitive Brief (2026-06-01)

**Run date:** 2026-06-01 | **Window:** 2026-05-25 → 2026-06-01 | **Baseline:** DEV-62 (2026-05-26)

---

### What It Builds
Full-stack web and mobile apps from natural language in a cloud IDE, with autonomous 200-min runs and real-browser self-testing. Agent 4 (2026) runs parallel artifact builds. This week shipped five new generation modalities: audio (music/SFX/speech), 3D models (Tripo3D), and Canvas-native media (images, vectors, videos). Also added Stripe one-click install from Marketplace (no API key copying required). Pivoting hard toward enterprise: Visa investment, $9B valuation, Solution Partner Program launched.

### Onboarding Flow
Prompt → live URL in <2 minutes. Plan-while-build mode (Agent 4): agent shows execution plan concurrently as it builds. Stripe Marketplace (May 29): one-click install, no API key copying. Design Canvas: Figma import → static site in <2 min. 30+ connectors platform.

### Pricing (2026-06-01)
| Plan | Price | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|
| Starter | $0 | Limited |
| Core | $20/mo (annual) | $20 credits included |
| Pro | $95/mo (annual) | 15 builders |
| Enterprise | Custom | SLA, SSO |

No changes from May 26 baseline.

### Strengths
1. **Five new generation modalities (May 29)** — Agent audio generation (music/SFX/speech), Tripo3D connector (3D from text/image), Canvas media generation (images/vectors/videos), Stripe one-click Marketplace install, Enterprise self-serve up to $200K. [Source: blog.replit.com, May 29 2026]
2. **Enterprise pivot signals** — Visa investment (May 28), Solution Partner Program (Accenture, Slalom, Hexaware); valuation $3B (Sep 2025) → $9B (Mar 2026); fastest-growing enterprise trajectory in this cohort. [Source: blog.replit.com]
3. **Connector-as-app-install pattern (Stripe Marketplace)** — one-click install eliminates API key copying friction; immediately applicable to any connector integration. [Source: blog.replit.com, May 29]

### Weaknesses
1. **Credit opacity** — effort-based pricing model; large builds are unpredictable in cost.
2. **Cloud-only** — no local/offline mode; existing repos with complex Git workflows are awkward.
3. **Plan-while-build vs plan-before-build** — shows plan concurrently but doesn't enforce approval gate before code runs.

### Patterns to Copy
- **One-click connector install (Marketplace)** — connectors install as apps with no API key copying; user grants permissions, not credentials. Directly applicable to our integration scaffolding.
- **Plan-while-build** — show the agent's execution plan to the user in real-time as the build proceeds, not as a blocking gate.
- **Audio/3D generation agents** — media-type-specific agents as first-class build outputs (not post-build steps).

### Patterns to Beat
- **Credit opacity** — our task-bounded credit model (transparent cost per task type) is a competitive wedge.
- **Cloud-only** — our repo-native stack runs anywhere; Replit requires cloud.

### Decision
**Adapt** — The connector-as-app-install pattern (Stripe Marketplace one-click) is immediately importable as an integration scaffolding improvement (S effort, high impact for client-facing app builds). Plan-while-build is worth adding to our build agent UX.

### Sources
- https://blog.replit.com (May 29, May 28 2026 posts)
- https://replit.com/pricing
- https://docs.replit.com/updates

### Diff vs 2026-05-26 Baseline
- **new_features:** Agent audio generation, Tripo3D connector, Canvas media generation (images/vectors/videos), Stripe one-click Marketplace install, Enterprise self-serve up to $200K (all May 29); Visa investment + Intelligent Commerce integration exploratory (May 28)
- **pricing_changes:** —
- **new_flows:** Stripe Marketplace one-click install (no API key copying); media generation modalities in Canvas
- **removed:** —
- **positioning:** Accelerating pivot from "AI coding playground" to "enterprise AI software factory"; Solution Partner Program with Accenture/Slalom/Hexaware signals enterprise GTM

## Input

Signal: 33 of 38 in_review issues are stale >48h (87%). The pattern has recurred across at least 3 consecutive weeks (confirmed in DEV-171, last week's Upgrade Report, and this week's DEV-211). Root cause: CEO quality-gate sweep dispatches QA Gate sub-issues but the in_review queue is never actively triaged or batch-closed. Autopilot-generated Daily Digest and Factory Health Audit issues accumulate with no cleanup cadence. No workspace memory captures this pattern for future CEO runs.

Score: 20 (Impact=4, Effort=S, Confidence=5)
Source: Workspace Upgrade Report 2026-06-07, DEV-211

## Expected Output

Two new memory entries in the workspace memory system:

1. **Pattern memory:** "In_review queue clogs weekly — CEO must proactively triage stale autopilot digest/audit issues, not just dispatch QA Gate sub-issues. As of 2026-06-07: 33 of 38 in_review issues are stale >48h. Autopilot-generated issues (Daily Digest, Factory Health Audit, Workspace Upgrade Report) with last_run_verdict=posted and no open children can be marked done without QA gate."

2. **Standing rule memory:** "Every CEO run: after dispatching QA Gate sub-issues, also sweep for autopilot digest/audit issues in in_review stale >7 days and close them directly (mark done). These are completed reports, not shippable work — they do not need a QA gate."

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Read the existing workspace memory index and confirm no duplicate entry exists
- [ ] Write memory entry 1 (pattern observation with date and count)
- [ ] Write memory entry 2 (standing CEO rule for digest/audit cleanup)
- [ ] Update the memory index (MEMORY.md) with both new entries
- [ ] Post a result comment on DEV-211 with the memory slugs and their locations

## Dependencies
- Access to workspace memory directory (already exists)
- Understanding of the CEO agent prompt and in_review sweep logic

## Risk
Low — memory entries are additive and do not change any code or agent prompts. They provide context that the CEO agent reads at the start of future runs.

## Definition of Done
- Two memory files written with correct frontmatter (type: feedback + type: project)
- MEMORY.md index updated
- Result comment posted on DEV-211
issues.title: Workspace Upgrade Action — Capture in_review queue-clog as standing workspace memory

## Rationale
ADR-001 (DEV-128) documents the default stack for web apps (Next.js + Supabase + Vercel) but makes no decision on the mobile layer. The Android Attendance app was built using Expo (React Native) with a local apk build path, but:
1. No ADR formalizes Expo as the default mobile stack
2. The android-apk-local-builder skill has retry_log=3 — three consecutive QA gate failures (DEV-174)
3. EAS Build (Expo's cloud CI) is deferred per local-first policy but not formally documented
4. There is no guidance on when to use React Native vs. a web PWA vs. a native Android/Kotlin app

Without a formal mobile layer decision, future app requests (e.g. HR mobile, delivery driver app) will diverge in stack choice.

## Evidence
- ADR-001 (DEV-128): no mobile layer defined
- DEV-43 (Android Attendance build): Expo + local Gradle build
- DEV-174 (Workspace Upgrade Action): android-apk-local-builder skill retry_log=3, in_review
- DEV-30 (Architecture: Android Attendance): Expo/React Native used without a formal ADR backing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ADR-001 updated (or an ADR-003 created) with a mobile layer section: default=Expo (React Native), build=EAS Build for production / local Gradle for dev, when to deviate (native-only requirement, AR/VR, heavy OS integration)
- [ ] Minimum viable mobile build checklist documented (env vars, signing, EAS profile)
- [ ] android-apk-local-builder skill fix (DEV-174) referenced as the validation path

## Definition of Done
- Mobile layer decision is in a signed ADR
- Solution Architect and CEO Command Squad have reviewed

## Effort: S

# SKILL: pre-build-research-gate

**Name:** `pre-build-research-gate`  
**Version:** 1.0.0 — 2026-06-01  
**Source:** Original — devosnt Skill Factory (DEV-187)

---

## Purpose

Before any build agent writes a line of code, spawn a lightweight research pass that surfaces comparable products, a differentiation claim, and the riskiest assumption in the issue brief. Outputs a 3-bullet **pre-build brief** as a comment on the build issue — closing the gap identified in the 2026-06-01 benchmark sweep where 4/6 competitors (Lovable Plan Mode, Replit Agent 4, Atoms.dev Iris, Cursor Auto-review v3.6) ship some form of planning-before-execution.

Use whenever a build sub-issue is about to start (status moving to `todo` or `in_progress`) and the description is ≥ 50 words. Works as a standalone invocation or as a pre-hook in the Build Squad workflow.

---

## Trigger

Invoke this skill when **all** of the following are true:

1. A build issue is about to transition to `in_progress` (or `todo` if invoked pre-emptively by the squad lead).
2. The issue description (or its parent issue description) is ≥ 50 words.
3. The issue lives in `05 - Parallel Build` or is otherwise tagged as a hands-on build task (schema, API, frontend, backend, mobile, integrations).

Do NOT invoke for QA issues, planning/spec issues, or issues that are research tasks themselves.

---

## Required Input

| Field | Source | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `issue_id` | Provided by caller | The build issue to brief |
| `gate_mode` | Workspace config or caller | `inform_only` (default) \| `auto_approve_after_60s` \| `approve` |
| Issue description | `multica issue get <issue_id>` | Must be ≥ 50 words; extracted automatically |
| Parent PRD (if any) | Parent issue or metadata | Optional — enriches research context |
| App name + target user | Derived from description | Used as research seed |

---

## Steps

### Step 1 — Context Extraction

1. Run `multica issue get <issue_id> --output json`.
2. Extract: **app name**, **core feature** being built, **target user segment**, **stack** (if mentioned), and any explicit business goal.
3. If the description is < 50 words, skip to **Failure Handling → Insufficient description** below.
4. If the issue has a parent, run `multica issue get <parent_id> --output json` and extract PRD context for richer research seeds.

### Step 2 — Comparable Products Research

Run a focused research pass (WebSearch, documentation, or internal knowledge) to identify 2–3 comparable apps or services that solve a similar problem for similar users.

Search framing: `"[app type] app for [user segment]"` — e.g. `"attendance app for hourly workers"`.

For each comparable, record:
- Product name (real, named product — no hypotheticals)
- One-sentence description of how it addresses the same use case
- Notable differentiator (good or bad)

If fewer than 2 real comparables can be found, flag as `insufficient_context` and proceed without blocking.

### Step 3 — Differentiation Claim

Based on the issue description and comparables found, formulate a single-sentence differentiation claim:
- What does this build offer that the comparables do not, or does better?
- Ground the claim in specifics from the issue brief (e.g. bilingual support, offline-first, role-based permissions, specific integration).

Avoid generic claims ("it will be better" / "more user-friendly"). If the issue brief does not provide enough signal to form a specific claim, state: "Differentiation unclear from current brief — recommend PRD review."

### Step 4 — Riskiest Assumption Identification

Read the issue's acceptance criteria, dependencies, and any explicit risk section. Identify the single assumption most likely to be wrong that would require significant rework if it proves false.

Risk categories to check (in order of typical impact):
1. **External dependency assumption** — "Firebase will be available / provisioned in time"
2. **User behavior assumption** — "Users will have reliable mobile internet during shifts"
3. **Technical feasibility assumption** — "pg_cron can handle concurrent jobs for 1,000+ employees"
4. **Data availability assumption** — "Employee shift data is already clean and consistent"
5. **Scope assumption** — "The feature is fully specced and won't change after build starts"

State the assumption as a falsifiable claim: "We assume X. If wrong, consequence Y."

### Step 5 — Compose and Post Pre-Build Brief

Format the 3-bullet brief exactly as:

```
**Pre-Build Brief — [ISSUE TITLE]**

1. **Comparable apps:** [App A] ([one-sentence description]), [App B] ([one-sentence description])[, App C if found].
2. **Our differentiation:** [Specific one-sentence claim grounded in the issue brief.]
3. **Riskiest assumption:** We assume [X]. If wrong, [consequence Y requiring rework].

Gate mode: [inform_only | auto_approve_after_60s | approve]
Skill: pre-build-research-gate v1.0.0
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <issue_id> --content "..."` (use `--content-file` for length safety).

### Step 6 — Store in Metadata

Run: `multica issue metadata set <issue_id> --key pre_build_brief --value "[bullet 1 | bullet 2 | bullet 3]"`

Use `|`-separated pipe delimiters to keep the value on one line. Store only the 3 bullets, not the header or gate-mode footer.

### Step 7 — Apply Gate Mode

| Gate mode | Action |
|---|---|
| `inform_only` | Post brief and continue immediately. No wait. |
| `auto_approve_after_60s` | Post brief with: "Auto-approving in 60 s unless CEO or owner replies `HOLD`." Wait 60 seconds. If no `HOLD` comment, continue. |
| `approve` | Post brief and run `multica issue status <issue_id> blocked` + `multica issue metadata set <issue_id> --key waiting_on --value "pre_build_approval"`. Do not proceed until CEO or owner posts `APPROVED` as a comment. |

Default is `inform_only`. Change to `approve` only after the skill has been validated on ≥ 5 issues and the workspace owner explicitly upgrades the default.

---

## Output

### Comment on the build issue (always posted)

```
**Pre-Build Brief — Push Notifications Integration**

1. **Comparable apps:** OneSignal (multi-channel push for web+mobile with a generous free tier), Firebase Cloud Messaging / direct (Google's own FCM, used by most Android push implementations), Knock (developer-focused notification orchestration with workflow builder).
2. **Our differentiation:** Bilingual Arabic/English payloads combined with pg_cron-native scheduling inside Supabase — no external notification SaaS required, keeping data residency fully within the tenant's Supabase project.
3. **Riskiest assumption:** We assume the Firebase project will be provisioned before the build agent starts. If wrong, the FCM Server Key will be absent from Supabase Vault and all notification Edge Functions will fail silently with no fallback path.

Gate mode: inform_only
Skill: pre-build-research-gate v1.0.0
```

### Metadata on the build issue

```
pre_build_brief = "Comparables: OneSignal, FCM, Knock | Differentiation: Bilingual payloads + pg_cron inside Supabase, no external SaaS | Riskiest assumption: Firebase provisioning must precede build start"
```

---

## Quality Checklist

Before posting the brief, verify:

- [ ] All 3 bullets present
- [ ] Comparable apps are real, named products (≥ 2 found)
- [ ] Differentiation claim is specific and grounded in the issue brief — not generic
- [ ] Riskiest assumption is falsifiable ("We assume X. If wrong, Y") — not a vague concern
- [ ] Brief stored in `pre_build_brief` metadata on the build issue
- [ ] Comment posted within 3 minutes of invocation
- [ ] Gate mode applied correctly per workspace config
- [ ] No secrets or credentials included in the brief

---

## Failure Handling

| Scenario | Action |
|---|---|
| Description < 50 words | Skip skill. Pin `pre_build_brief = "skipped: description too short (<50 words)"` in metadata. Do not block the build. |
| Research returns < 2 real comparable apps | Post: "**Pre-Build Brief — [TITLE]** — Insufficient context for comparable apps. Proceeding without research gate." Pin `pre_build_brief = "insufficient_context"`. Do not block build. |
| `approve` gate + no response after 24 h | Escalate to CEO Command Squad. Post comment on issue: "Pre-build gate awaiting approval for 24 h — escalating to CEO." Do not auto-unblock. |
| Metadata write fails | Continue anyway. Brief is durable in the comment. Log the failure in the comment footer: "(metadata write failed — brief in comment only)". |
| Parent issue read fails | Continue with build issue description alone. Note in brief footer: "(parent PRD unavailable — research based on build issue only)". |
| `multica issue comment add` fails | Retry once with `--content-file` (write to `/tmp/brief.md`). If still fails, post a shortened version (< 500 chars). |

---

## Improvement Rule

After every 5 invocations, skill-builder reviews:

1. Were comparable apps accurate and relevant? If > 50% were off-target, tighten the search framing guidance in Step 2.
2. Was the riskiest assumption actually the riskiest, post-build? If not, add the missed risk category to the Step 4 checklist.
3. Did the differentiation claim match what the build actually delivered? If consistently wrong, add a "check PRD for explicit differentiators" step before Step 3.
4. Was the gate mode used correctly? If `approve` was never used, consider removing it or downgrading to opt-in.
5. Bump version, post revision note on this issue.

Retire this skill if the Multica platform adds a native "plan before build" step that generates equivalent output automatically.

---

## Integration Notes

### As a pre-hook in agentic-codegen-loop

Add to the Build Squad workflow header:

```
Before writing any code:
1. Invoke `pre-build-research-gate` on this issue.
2. Read the resulting `pre_build_brief` metadata.
3. If the riskiest assumption is a hard external blocker (credential, paid resource), set issue to `blocked` and apply `external-blocker-escalation` before proceeding.
```

### As a standalone invocation

Any agent can invoke by running this skill on any issue ID that meets the trigger criteria. No squad-specific setup required.

---

## Benchmark Rationale (from DEV-187 / DEV-180)

| Competitor | Their approach | Our implementation |
|---|---|---|
| Lovable Plan Mode (Feb 2026) | Shows approach for user approval before any code runs | `approve` gate mode (opt-in) |
| Replit Agent 4 | Shows execution plan concurrently as build proceeds | `inform_only` mode (default) |
| Atoms.dev Iris | Market research + competitive analysis before code runs | Step 2 comparable research |
| Cursor Auto-review v3.6 | Classifier subagent triages calls before execution | Step 4 riskiest assumption gate |
issues.title: SKILL: pre-build-research-gate — research + differentiation gate before any build starts

## Cursor (agent mode) — Competitive Brief (2026-06-01)

**Run date:** 2026-06-01 | **Window:** 2026-05-25 → 2026-06-01 | **Baseline:** DEV-62 (2026-05-26)

---

### What It Builds
An AI-native IDE (VS Code fork) with full-codebase agent mode for developers. Agents autonomously edit files across multiple repos, run terminal commands, browse the web, and visually verify UI via Computer Use. Background cloud agents process GitHub issues and Slack messages into draft PRs with no laptop needed. v3.6 (May 29) adds Auto-review Run Mode — a classifier subagent that triages Shell/MCP/Fetch calls into allowlist / sandbox / human-escalation buckets before execution.

### Onboarding Flow
Install Cursor IDE → connect GitHub repo → prompt agent. Background/Cloud Agents: input a GitHub issue or Slack message, receive a draft PR. Auto-review Run Mode: enabled per-project, classifier subagent runs before any Shell/MCP/Fetch call. Microsoft Teams: @Cursor in any Teams channel to delegate to a cloud agent (May 11).

### Pricing (2026-06-01)
| Plan | Price | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|
| Hobby (Free) | $0 | Basic completions |
| Pro | $20/mo | Full agent access |
| Pro+ | $60/mo | Higher limits |
| Ultra | $200/mo | Highest limits |
| Teams | $40/user/mo | Team features |

Base tier pricing unchanged. BugBot pricing changed: **from $40/seat/month flat → usage-based ~$1–1.50/run**, effective June 8, 2026. Configurable effort levels added.

### Strengths
1. **Auto-review Run Mode v3.6 (May 29)** — Classifier subagent triages Shell/MCP/Fetch calls into allowlist/sandbox/human-escalation before execution. First safety layer for agentic code execution in an IDE. [Source: cursor.com/changelog]
2. **Parallel /multitask + BugBot** — Spawns parallel async subagents per independent task branch; BugBot auto-reviews and fixes ~80% of PR issues autonomously. [Source: cursor.com/blog]
3. **Microsoft Teams integration (May 11)** — @Cursor in any Teams channel delegates to cloud agent; enterprise distribution play. [Source: cursor.com/changelog]

### Weaknesses
1. **Context window gap** — Effective context 70–120K (not marketed 200K); large repos hit limits on complex multi-file changes.
2. **IDE lock-in** — VS Code fork limits portability; not all JetBrains/Vim users will switch.
3. **Agent cost premium** — Runs cost 12–80% more than CLI alternatives at scale.

### Patterns to Copy
- **Classifier-gated autonomous execution** — before any Shell/MCP/Fetch call, a lightweight classifier subagent triages: allowlist (execute immediately) / sandbox (execute with restrictions) / human-escalation. Directly applicable to our build agents.
- **Effort-level configurability** — let users choose how deeply agents investigate before acting (fast/balanced/thorough), not just model selection.

### Patterns to Beat
- **IDE lock-in** — our agents work in any repo/environment; Cursor requires their specific IDE.
- **Funding-stage pricing risk** — $2B raise at $50B+ valuation means pricing pressure is coming; our cost model should stay transparent.

### Decision
**Study only** — Auto-review classifier pattern (triage before execution) is the most architecturally sound idea from this brief and directly importable as a prompt/rule layer in our build agents. BugBot's usage-based pricing change is worth monitoring as a signal.

### Sources
- https://www.cursor.com/changelog
- https://cursor.com/blog/cursor-3
- https://cursor.com/pricing

### Diff vs 2026-05-26 Baseline
- **new_features:** v3.6 Auto-review Run Mode (May 29) — classifier subagent triages Shell/MCP/Fetch calls before execution
- **pricing_changes:** BugBot changed from $40/seat/month → usage-based ~$1–1.50/run, effective June 8, 2026
- **new_flows:** Auto-review pre-execution triage; Microsoft Teams integration for cloud agents (May 11)
- **removed:** —
- **positioning:** "AI code editor" → "the best coding agent"; framed as "unified workspace for building software with agents"

issues.description:
Daily Command Center Digest. Mode=create_issue. Runs every day 07:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Produces ONE per-project status briefing comment on the dated issue this autopilot creates, so the CEO + workspace owner can see — at a glance — every project's state and what to push today.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

(5) Render the digest comment using this exact shape (parseable by humans and future audits):

```
# Daily Command Center Digest — <YYYY-MM-DD KW>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC) | **Projects scanned:** N | **Open issues:** N | **Blocked:** N | **Stale in_review (48h):** N | **Approval asks:** N

## Per-Project Status

### <project icon> <project title>
- **Goal:** <one sentence>
- **Phase:** <lifecycle_phase>
- **Active:** todo=<n> in_progress=<n> in_review=<n> blocked=<n>
- **Blockers / waiting_on:** <list or "none">
- **Next action:** <DEV-NN title — owner=name>
- **Risk:** <one line>
- **Approval needed:** <yes/no> [— "<exact ask>"]

### <next project>
… (same shape)

## Top 3 to Push Today

1. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
2. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
3. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>

## Deltas Since Yesterday
- New blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Cleared blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New approval asks: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Stale in_review (48h+): <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New app requests: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Lifecycle phase changes: <project: old→new or "none">
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body). NEVER include any `mention://agent/...` or `mention://member/...` link. Issue references should be plain `DEV-NN` text (no `mention://issue/...` either — keep the digest scannable).

(6) On a green day (step 2 short-circuited), do NOT post any comment. Skip to step (8).

(7) Pin metadata on THIS issue only if a value materially helps tomorrow's run:
    - `last_run_verdict` = "green" or "posted" (overwrite each run).
    - `last_window_end` = <RFC3339-now> (overwrite each run).
    - `lifecycle_phase_snapshot` = JSON map `{project_id: phase}` so step (2) can detect phase changes tomorrow.
    - `top3_count` = number (expect 0 on green days, 3 otherwise).
    Use `multica issue metadata set` once per changed key. Skip any key whose value is identical to what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Status finalization.
    - If a digest was posted: flip THIS issue to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). CEO + owner read it, then close.
    - If green day (no digest posted): flip THIS issue to `cancelled` with no comment so it stops cluttering the inbox.

(9) Output one line to stdout (only non-comment output): `Daily Digest @ <RFC3339> | verdict=<green|posted> | projects=N | top3=N | blocked=B stale=S approvals=A`.

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI failure. Log "skipped <signal>: <reason>" and continue with the remaining signals.
- All comments go ONLY on the issue this autopilot creates. Never comment on any other issue.
- Never mention any agent or member anywhere. Plain names only.
- Read this issue's existing metadata before pinning. Only update keys whose values changed.
- Do not create sub-issues, do not reassign any other issue, do not change any other issue's status.
- The created issue MUST land in `in_review` (when posted) or `cancelled` (green day) — never `done` (owner closes), never left in `todo`.

---
*Autopilot run triggered at 2026-06-08 07:00 Asia/Kuwait. After starting work, rename this issue to accurately reflect what you are doing.*
issues.title: Daily Command Center Digest — 2026-06-08

## Input

Signal: 17 blocked issues, 7+ waiting on admin credentials (GH PAT, FIGMA_TOKEN, Context7 API key, Sentry tokens, codegraph install) for 7–12 days. Issues: DEV-93, DEV-96, DEV-97, DEV-106, DEV-108, DEV-126, DEV-169. Multiple escalation comments have been posted (via external-blocker-escalation skill) but none have produced an admin response. The current escalation pattern posts per-issue separate asks, which fragments the admin's action surface and reduces follow-through.

Score: 12.5 (Impact=5, Effort=S, Confidence=5)
Source: Workspace Upgrade Report 2026-06-07, DEV-211

A prompt patch for the `external-blocker-escalation` skill (or a new prompt template for agents that post escalations) that:

2. Includes a concrete deadline ask ("Please respond by YYYY-MM-DD or the following features remain blocked").
3. Lists a free-tier/workaround fallback for each credential so admin can unblock the item even without paid resources.
4. Adds a single "Reply to this comment to unblock" call-to-action.
5. Removes redundant per-issue escalation noise.

The patch should be applied to the `external-blocker-escalation` skill definition and/or the `ceo` agent prompt (the in_review sweep section).

- Access to ceo agent prompt (for the in_review sweep section)

## Risk
Medium — changing the CEO escalation prompt could affect how other escalations are handled. Mitigate by: only modifying the escalation comment template, not the routing or status-flip logic.

## Definition of Done
- external-blocker-escalation skill updated with consolidated escalation template
- One consolidated escalation comment posted covering all 7+ admin credential blockers
- Summary posted on DEV-211

## SKILL IMPORT: ab-test-analysis

**Source:** https://github.com/VoltAgent/awesome-claude-code-subagents/blob/main/categories/10-research-analysis/ab-test-analysis.md
**License:** MIT ✓
**Stars:** 20,988 (VoltAgent/awesome-claude-code-subagents)

## What it does
Analyzes A/B test results with statistical rigour — p-values, effect size (not just significance), statistical power, novelty effects, and multiple comparison corrections. Outputs a structured ship/no-ship recommendation with confidence level.

## Proposed Multica skill name
`ab-test-analysis`

## PR-ready spec
- **Purpose:** Interpret A/B experiment results and produce a principled ship/no-ship decision
- **Trigger:** User wants to analyze A/B test results, asks about p-value, statistical significance, confidence interval, 'did the test work', 'should we ship this'
- **Input:** Control metrics (sample size, conversion rate, mean), variant metrics, test duration, hypothesis
- **Steps:** (1) Calculate statistical significance (two-tailed t-test or chi-square); (2) Calculate effect size and practical significance; (3) Check for novelty effect (test duration < 2 weeks?); (4) Check sample ratio mismatch; (5) Apply Bonferroni correction if multiple metrics; (6) Output verdict: SHIP / NO-SHIP / EXTEND with confidence
- **Output:** Significance verdict, effect size, power check, novelty risk flag, final recommendation
- **Quality checklist:** Effect size always reported alongside p-value, sample ratio mismatch checked, recommendation is binary (no 'it depends' copout)
- **Tools:** Read, Grep

## Distinct from existing skill
`ab-testing-framework` sets up the test infrastructure (PostHog, feature flags, sample size calculator). This skill analyses results after the test runs. Different phase, no overlap.

## Acceptance Criteria
- SKILL.md written per Multica skill format
- Covers: p-value, effect size, power, novelty effect, multiple comparisons
- Triggers on: 'A/B result', 'statistical significance', 'ship or no ship', 'p-value'
- Output always includes a binary recommendation

## Dependencies
- Security review sub-issue must be in `done` with `decision=green` before this is promoted to `todo`

## Risk
- Low: MIT license, pure analytical instructions, no external calls

## Definition of Done
- [ ] SKILL.md authored and merged to skill library
- [ ] Skill registered in workspace
- [ ] Trigger tested on 2 sample prompts (e-commerce and SaaS)
- [ ] Output includes binary ship/no-ship in all test cases

## Bolt.new — Competitive Brief (2026-06-01)

**Run date:** 2026-06-01 | **Window:** 2026-05-25 → 2026-06-01 | **Baseline:** DEV-62 (2026-05-26)

---

### What It Builds
React/Vite/TypeScript web apps generated entirely in-browser via StackBlitz WebContainers, with Expo-based iOS/Android from a single prompt. The AI has full filesystem, Node server, package manager, terminal, and browser console control. Design System Agents (May 29) now let teams ingest their own design system (npm package, Storybook, or repo) so Bolt generates production-fidelity code using real tokens and real components instead of hallucinated elements.

### Onboarding Flow
Zero-install browser session → prompt → live app. Dual agent system: Standard (Sonnet 4.6, balanced) for routine builds; Max (Opus 4.6, deep reasoning) for complex projects. Design System Agents activated by connecting npm package/Storybook/repo. Real-time multi-user collaboration live since Apr 2026.

### Pricing (2026-06-01)
| Plan | Price | Tokens/mo | Notes |
|------|-------|-----------|-------|
| Free | $0 | 1M | Bolt branding on sites |
| Pro | $25/mo | 10M+ | Token rollover, custom domains, AI image edit |
| Teams | $30/member/mo | 10M+/member | Private NPM, Design Systems, admin controls |
| Enterprise | Custom | Custom | SSO, audit logs, SLAs |

No changes from May 26 baseline. "MaxPro" appearing in UI — likely internal beta label, not a published tier.

### Strengths
1. **Design System Agents (May 29)** — Ingest team's npm package, Storybook, or repo; Bolt generates using real design tokens and real components. Closes the "Bolt generates ugly generic UIs" complaint. [Source: bolt.new blog, May 29 2026]
2. **OSS surface** — `stackblitz-labs/bolt.diy` (MIT, 15+ LLM providers, 19K+ stars) enables self-hosted/custom-model deployments — no competitor has this. [Source: github.com/stackblitz-labs/bolt.diy]
3. **Zero-install + in-browser** — No local setup, no VM spin-up latency; entire stack runs in WebContainer. [Source: bolt.new]

### Weaknesses
1. **Token wall** — High token burn on complex apps; free tier exhausts quickly; v1 hard sunset Aug 3 2026 creates migration trust gap.
2. **Positioning drift** — "98% less errors" and "1,000x larger projects" claims without published benchmarks undermine credibility.
3. **Deployment gap** — Bolt V2 Cloud is newer; enterprise teams may still need external infra.

### Patterns to Copy
- **Design system ingest** — accept a team's npm package or Storybook and use real tokens/components in generated code instead of generic defaults.
- **Dual-agent routing** — route routine tasks to a fast/cheap model (Sonnet 4.6) and complex tasks to a deep-reasoning model (Opus 4.6) automatically, not manually.

### Patterns to Beat
- **v1 hard sunset (Aug 3, 2026)** — forced migrations erode trust; our rolling skill-upgrade approach keeps backward compatibility.
- **Token opacity** — users don't know how many tokens a build will cost; our credit model should be transparent and bounded per task type.

### Decision
**Adapt** — Design System Agents and dual-agent routing are worth implementing. Design system ingest is a high-impact, low-effort skill (S effort, direct win-rate improvement for design-led clients). The v1 migration trust failure is an exploitable competitive gap.

### Sources
- https://bolt.new
- https://bolt.new/blog (Design System Agents, May 29 2026)
- https://github.com/stackblitz-labs/bolt.diy
- https://bolt.new/pricing

### Diff vs 2026-05-26 Baseline
- **new_features:** Design System Agents (May 29) — ingest npm/Storybook/repo for production-fidelity code generation
- **pricing_changes:** —
- **new_flows:** Design System Agent onboarding (connect design system before build)
- **removed:** "Open in StackBlitz" export no longer available for new projects
- **positioning:** Speed-first → reliability/scale ("98% less errors," "1,000x larger projects"); "MaxPro" internal beta label appearing in UI

issues.description:
Daily Command Center Digest. Mode=create_issue. Runs every day 07:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Produces ONE per-project status briefing comment on the dated issue this autopilot creates, so the CEO + workspace owner can see — at a glance — every project's state and what to push today.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

(5) Render the digest comment using this exact shape (parseable by humans and future audits):

```
# Daily Command Center Digest — <YYYY-MM-DD KW>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC) | **Projects scanned:** N | **Open issues:** N | **Blocked:** N | **Stale in_review (48h):** N | **Approval asks:** N

## Per-Project Status

### <project icon> <project title>
- **Goal:** <one sentence>
- **Phase:** <lifecycle_phase>
- **Active:** todo=<n> in_progress=<n> in_review=<n> blocked=<n>
- **Blockers / waiting_on:** <list or "none">
- **Next action:** <DEV-NN title — owner=name>
- **Risk:** <one line>
- **Approval needed:** <yes/no> [— "<exact ask>"]

### <next project>
… (same shape)

## Top 3 to Push Today

1. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
2. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
3. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>

## Deltas Since Yesterday
- New blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Cleared blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New approval asks: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Stale in_review (48h+): <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New app requests: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Lifecycle phase changes: <project: old→new or "none">
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body). NEVER include any `mention://agent/...` or `mention://member/...` link. Issue references should be plain `DEV-NN` text (no `mention://issue/...` either — keep the digest scannable).

(6) On a green day (step 2 short-circuited), do NOT post any comment. Skip to step (8).

(7) Pin metadata on THIS issue only if a value materially helps tomorrow's run:
    - `last_run_verdict` = "green" or "posted" (overwrite each run).
    - `last_window_end` = <RFC3339-now> (overwrite each run).
    - `lifecycle_phase_snapshot` = JSON map `{project_id: phase}` so step (2) can detect phase changes tomorrow.
    - `top3_count` = number (expect 0 on green days, 3 otherwise).
    Use `multica issue metadata set` once per changed key. Skip any key whose value is identical to what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Status finalization.
    - If a digest was posted: flip THIS issue to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). CEO + owner read it, then close.
    - If green day (no digest posted): flip THIS issue to `cancelled` with no comment so it stops cluttering the inbox.

(9) Output one line to stdout (only non-comment output): `Daily Digest @ <RFC3339> | verdict=<green|posted> | projects=N | top3=N | blocked=B stale=S approvals=A`.

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI failure. Log "skipped <signal>: <reason>" and continue with the remaining signals.
- All comments go ONLY on the issue this autopilot creates. Never comment on any other issue.
- Never mention any agent or member anywhere. Plain names only.
- Read this issue's existing metadata before pinning. Only update keys whose values changed.
- Do not create sub-issues, do not reassign any other issue, do not change any other issue's status.
- The created issue MUST land in `in_review` (when posted) or `cancelled` (green day) — never `done` (owner closes), never left in `todo`.

Run the `workspace-upgrade-report` skill against the live workspace and post a single ranked action list as a comment on the issue this autopilot creates. Auto-dispatch the top 3 actions to skill-builder, prompt-optimizer, and memory-curator. Never skip a week — a silent skip is itself a finding.

STEPS:

(1) Scope the scan window. Treat `since = now - 7 days` (RFC3339, UTC). Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

(2) Collect the five input signals:

   (a) retry_logs (last 7 days). Walk every issue updated in the past 7 days and pull metadata. Any `retry_log` key present is a failure signature. Aggregate by skill name / agent / failure_reason.
       - `multica issue list --output json --status in_progress` and `--status blocked` and `--status in_review` (paged if needed).
       - For each issue, `multica issue metadata list <id> --output json` — keep only entries with a `retry_log` key.

   (b) Blocked issues — every issue currently `status=blocked`:
       - `multica issue list --status blocked --output json`
       - For each: capture id, identifier, title, assignee, age (now - updated_at), and `blocked_reason` metadata.

   (c) Stale in_review (>48h) — every issue currently `status=in_review` whose `updated_at` is more than 48h ago:
       - `multica issue list --status in_review --output json`
       - Filter client-side by `updated_at`.

   (d) Agent WIP imbalance — for every agent in the workspace, count `in_progress` issues assigned to them. Flag any agent with WIP=0 (cold) or WIP≥4 (overloaded). Flag any squad whose total WIP ≥7.

   (e) Skill orphans — every skill not assigned to any agent (`agent_count==0`), and every agent with zero skills attached.
       - `multica skill list --output json`
       - For each skill, attempt `multica skill get <id> --output json` (or inspect the skill→agent join if exposed). If the CLI exposes no join command, infer from agent definitions (`multica agent get <id> --output json` per agent) — cap the scan at 30 agents per run.

(3) Score and rank actions. For each candidate upgrade action, score on:
   - Impact (how many issues/agents this would unblock or accelerate; 1–5)

```
## Next Steps
```

issues.description:
Daily Command Center Digest. Mode=create_issue. Runs every day 07:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Produces ONE per-project status briefing comment on the dated issue this autopilot creates, so the CEO + workspace owner can see — at a glance — every project's state and what to push today.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

(5) Render the digest comment using this exact shape (parseable by humans and future audits):

```
# Daily Command Center Digest — <YYYY-MM-DD KW>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC) | **Projects scanned:** N | **Open issues:** N | **Blocked:** N | **Stale in_review (48h):** N | **Approval asks:** N

## Per-Project Status

### <project icon> <project title>
- **Goal:** <one sentence>
- **Phase:** <lifecycle_phase>
- **Active:** todo=<n> in_progress=<n> in_review=<n> blocked=<n>
- **Blockers / waiting_on:** <list or "none">
- **Next action:** <DEV-NN title — owner=name>
- **Risk:** <one line>
- **Approval needed:** <yes/no> [— "<exact ask>"]

### <next project>
… (same shape)

## Top 3 to Push Today

1. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
2. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
3. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>

## Deltas Since Yesterday
- New blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Cleared blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New approval asks: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Stale in_review (48h+): <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New app requests: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Lifecycle phase changes: <project: old→new or "none">
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body). NEVER include any `mention://agent/...` or `mention://member/...` link. Issue references should be plain `DEV-NN` text (no `mention://issue/...` either — keep the digest scannable).

(6) On a green day (step 2 short-circuited), do NOT post any comment. Skip to step (8).

(7) Pin metadata on THIS issue only if a value materially helps tomorrow's run:
    - `last_run_verdict` = "green" or "posted" (overwrite each run).
    - `last_window_end` = <RFC3339-now> (overwrite each run).
    - `lifecycle_phase_snapshot` = JSON map `{project_id: phase}` so step (2) can detect phase changes tomorrow.
    - `top3_count` = number (expect 0 on green days, 3 otherwise).
    Use `multica issue metadata set` once per changed key. Skip any key whose value is identical to what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Status finalization.
    - If a digest was posted: flip THIS issue to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). CEO + owner read it, then close.
    - If green day (no digest posted): flip THIS issue to `cancelled` with no comment so it stops cluttering the inbox.

(9) Output one line to stdout (only non-comment output): `Daily Digest @ <RFC3339> | verdict=<green|posted> | projects=N | top3=N | blocked=B stale=S approvals=A`.

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI failure. Log "skipped <signal>: <reason>" and continue with the remaining signals.
- All comments go ONLY on the issue this autopilot creates. Never comment on any other issue.
- Never mention any agent or member anywhere. Plain names only.
- Read this issue's existing metadata before pinning. Only update keys whose values changed.
- Do not create sub-issues, do not reassign any other issue, do not change any other issue's status.
- The created issue MUST land in `in_review` (when posted) or `cancelled` (green day) — never `done` (owner closes), never left in `todo`.

---

Monthly Architecture & Tool-stack Review — keeps ADRs current, default tech stack honest, and per-integration posture fresh. Runs on the 1st of each month at 11:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3) — Multica's cron parser does not support "nth weekday of month", so day-1 was chosen over "every Sunday + self-gate" to avoid spawning 3 auto-cancelled issues per month. Mode=create_issue (project: 00 - Factory Brain, id 1a1c2fb3-41ee-4b99-b820-d10f1c564052).

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do NOT create additional issues except the up-to-5 architecture-action sub-issues in step (7).
- NEVER mention the CEO (d9f942b9-e245-4e28-99eb-8f12fbebc2c3) in any comment — assignment alone is the trigger.
- NEVER mention any agent in a wrap-up or thank-you. Only mention an agent on dispatch sub-issues, and only if assignment alone is insufficient.
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (the workspace-operations dialog).
- Read each issue's metadata before pinning anything; do not log run state.

REVIEW WINDOW:
- `since = first day of previous calendar month, 00:00 Asia/Kuwait` → `now`. Use RFC3339 UTC for any CLI/API filter.

INPUTS TO PULL (continue on per-source failure — log "skipped <source>: <reason>" to stdout):
- All ADRs in `00 - Factory Brain` (title prefix "ADR-" or label "adr"). `multica issue list --project 1a1c2fb3-41ee-4b99-b820-d10f1c564052 --output json`.
- All apps shipped or in-flight in the window (projects whose titles start with "APP —").
- Default stack ground-truth: scan `multica skill list --output json` plus the workspace CLAUDE.md content surfaced in your runtime brief.
- Per-runtime cost / quota notes: latest "Workspace Upgrade Weekly Loop" issue + comments for the trailing month (project 00 - Factory Brain).
- Per-integration health: upstream release notes via `gh` CLI / public RSS / vendor changelogs. Never use `curl`/`wget` against Multica.
- Open CVEs: GitHub Security Advisories (`gh api /advisories?ecosystem=...`) for Node, Go, Postgres, Next.js, Expo, plus declared SDKs.

STEPS:

(1) ADR audit
   - List every ADR-* issue (any status).
   - For each, classify: still-load-bearing | superseded (cite successor) | contradicted (cite the shipped app / decision that diverged) | stale.
   - Cross-reference each shipped app's PRD / schema / API contract to detect contradictions.
   - Output as a table.

(2) Default tech stack
   - Restate the current default stack per layer (frontend, backend, DB, auth, deploy, mobile, payments, messaging, analytics).
   - For each layer: (a) what matured in the past 30 days (releases, GA milestones), (b) any alternative now worth a re-look, (c) recommendation: hold | watch | re-evaluate.

(3) Per-runtime delta
   - Runtimes in scope: Claude Code, Codex, Opencode, Openclaw, Hermes.
   - For each: pricing / quota changes, new capabilities, deprecations, observed failure rate this month (cite any `agent-self-healing-policy` retry_log surfaced on issues).

(4) Per-integration delta
   - Integrations: Supabase, Vercel, KNet, MyFatoorah, Meta (FB/IG/WhatsApp Graph), Google (Maps/OAuth/Workspace), Expo.
   - For each: pricing changes, breaking API changes, new quotas / rate limits, deprecation notices. One bullet per integration. If nothing landed, write "no changes".

(5) Security posture
   - New CVE classes affecting our stack.
   - RLS / secret-handling gaps surfaced this month (cross-reference any `security-review-checklist` failures in `06 - QA Security Performance`).
   - One-paragraph posture summary + bulleted gaps.

(6) Top 5 architecture changes to consider
   - Derive from steps (1)–(5). Rank by impact × urgency. For each: title, rationale (1–2 lines), owner (squad / agent), effort (S/M/L), risk if skipped.
   - List fewer than 5 if fewer are warranted — do NOT invent fillers.
   - If the month is genuinely quiet, list 0 actions and post the comment marker "no changes — green".

(7) Auto-dispatch the Top 5 (or fewer) as sub-issues
   - For each action, create a sub-issue:
     - Title: "ARCH ACTION: <one-line>"
     - Parent: this autopilot's issue id
     - Project: 00 - Factory Brain (1a1c2fb3-41ee-4b99-b820-d10f1c564052)
     - Priority: high for security / CVE; medium for stack or runtime drift; low for "watch" items
     - Status: todo
     - Assignee: solution-architect (77da9a0d-924e-4087-9278-59ba1182b599) by default; database items → database-architect (89c4ed8b-e38b-48c4-9029-19fd7007a255); API contract items → api-architect (614bc86d-6ef1-4f1e-8b3c-b393b43f46d0); integration items → integration-architect (4d62192f-7508-4ed2-99f5-db8b3dabbd9c); security items → security-auditor (17759d29-f9bb-4c4c-910c-b4476723db87).
     - Description includes: rationale, evidence (links to ADR / app / CVE / release notes), Acceptance Criteria, DoD, suggested effort.
   - If the month is "green" (0 actions), skip dispatch but still complete steps (8)–(10).

(8) Post the audit report as ONE comment on this autopilot's created issue. Use this exact shape (HEREDOC via `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin`):


## SKILL IMPORT: hipaa-compliance-checklist

**Source:** https://github.com/VoltAgent/awesome-claude-code-subagents/blob/main/categories/07-specialized-domains/hipaa-compliance.md
**License:** MIT ✓
**Stars:** 20,988 (VoltAgent/awesome-claude-code-subagents)

## What it does
Guides engineering and product teams through HIPAA obligations for SaaS Business Associates: BAA requirements, PHI safeguards (technical, administrative, physical), breach notification windows, audit trail requirements, and HITECH extensions.

## Proposed Multica skill name
`hipaa-compliance-checklist`

## PR-ready spec
- **Purpose:** HIPAA gap analysis for healthcare tech products — determines BA vs CE status, required safeguards, missing controls
- **Trigger:** User mentions HIPAA, PHI, protected health information, healthcare compliance, clinic app, BAA, HITECH, health data
- **Input:** App feature list, data model (what patient data is stored), deployment environment
- **Steps:** (1) Determine if app qualifies as Business Associate; (2) Generate BAA requirement checklist; (3) Audit technical safeguards (encryption at rest/transit, access controls, audit logs); (4) Audit administrative safeguards (training, policies, incident response); (5) Flag gaps with priority (blocking vs. nice-to-have)
- **Output:** BA/CE classification, BAA checklist, gap matrix (blocking/non-blocking), minimum viable compliance path
- **Quality checklist:** All 3 safeguard categories covered, breach notification window cited (72h for HITECH), no legal advice claimed
- **Tools:** Read, Grep

## Distinct from existing skills
We have `pdpl-kuwait-compliance` (Kuwait), `gdpr-compliance-checklist` (EU), `soc2-readiness-checklist` (SOC2). HIPAA is unaddressed and is directly relevant to `clinic-system-builder` workloads.

## Acceptance Criteria
- SKILL.md written per Multica skill format
- Covers: BAA, PHI technical safeguards, administrative safeguards, breach notification
- Triggers on: HIPAA, PHI, healthcare compliance, clinic, BAA
- Does not provide legal advice (includes disclaimer)
